title: ranunculus abortivus subsp. acrolasius
intro: Ranunculus abortivus subsp. acrolasius, also known as buttercups, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ranunculus abortivus subsp. acrolasius include reducing inflammation and promoting healing, and it has also been used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and is also used in topical applications such as salves and creams.
